text:
She was a shepherd girl living outside a small village. Every morning she’d leave her home to tend to her flock. By midday, she’d lazily graze them among the hills. It was dusk when she found her favorite pasture—a lush green valley. There she’d usually spot her dog, Lucky.

One evening, as usual, they trotted toward the valley. Lucky barked excitedly. There, on the hillside, sat a large, beautiful old oak tree. Its branches seemed to shelter a graceful deer. They approached slowly.

Curiosity made her pet hop ahead. The deer paused gracefully. She extended her antlered antebellum-style hat just enough for the girl to peek into the ornery eye slits.

"Oh hello there… Have you seen a girl with a big brown dog lately?"

They eyed each other warily.

"It’s me. Just came across the pond today. Aren’t your antlers pretty?"

She smiled warmly. The deer’s ant
